dashboard.allClear: Everything looks good — no issues detected.
dashboard.noOrgDesc: Create your first org to start tracking.
dashboard.getStartedConnectDesc: Download the ACTV TRKR plugin, install it on WordPress, and paste your API key to start tracking.

dashboard.reviewSeoIssues: Review SEO issues to improve visibility
dashboard.pluginCronStuck: Plugin may not be polling — check WP cron
dashboard.positiveSignals: Positive signals will appear here as data is collected.
dashboard.allCaughtUpDesc: You've used all your AI insights for today. Fresh insights will be ready for you tomorrow morning. ☕
dashboard.usedAllInsights: You've used all your AI insights for today. They'll refresh tomorrow!
dashboard.latestSummaryPreparing: Your nightly summary is being prepared. Once ACTV TRKR has a full day of tracking data, this section will show a concise recap of sessions, leads, and conversion trends — along with actionable insights to guide your next steps.
dashboard.ensureTracking: Ensure your tracking snippet is installed and receiving pageviews
dashboard.summariesRunNightly: Summaries run nightly and will appear here automatically
dashboard.visitReports: Visit Reports for deeper analysis once data is available
dashboard.siteCvrTooltip: Submissions as a percentage of total site sessions — not form-specific page views
dashboard.engagementDataPending: No engagement data for this period yet — metrics build up as real visitors browse your pages.
dashboard.clickDataPending: Click data will appear once behavioral tracking is active.
dashboard.welcomeBanner: Welcome! Let's get your site connected
dashboard.welcomeBannerDesc: Follow our 3-step guide to install the plugin, connect your API key, and start seeing real-time data.
dashboard.performanceStable: Performance stable. No material movement detected.

reports.title: Reports
reports.noDataYet: ACTV TRKR is collecting activity data. Insights will appear once enough data is available.
reports.buildingWeekly: Building your weekly summary from live tracking data…
reports.noWeeklySummary: No weekly summary record was found yet for this workspace.
reports.clickToGenerate: Click below to generate it now from your tracked sessions and leads.
reports.generateAiSummary: Generate an AI summary to see a plain-English recap of this week.
reports.reviewLeadSources: Review top lead sources for any declines or attribution changes.
reports.trafficGrowNoLeads: Traffic grew but leads didn't keep pace — check conversion paths on top landing pages.
reports.reviewOverviewTab: Review the Overview tab for detailed insights and suggested actions.

reports.noMonthlyData: Not enough data yet to generate a monthly summary.

reports.seoInsightsEmpty: SEO insights will appear once you run your first scan.

reports.fixQueued: Fix queued — your site plugin will apply it shortly
reports.issueMarkedFixed: Issue marked as fixed — it won't appear on future scans for this page
reports.fixReQueued: Fix re-queued — your plugin will pick it up on the next poll cycle
reports.pluginNotPolling: Plugin may not be polling — deactivate & reactivate in WP
reports.dailyLimitReached: Daily AI report limit reached. Try again tomorrow.
reports.toggleMetrics: Toggle individual metrics within this section:
reports.customizeDesc: Customize which sections and metrics appear in your PDF reports. Drag to reorder.
reports.changesApply: Changes apply to new reports you generate. Previously generated reports are not affected.

forms.title: Forms & Leads
forms.syncMayBeIncomplete: Sync may be incomplete until updated
forms.noFormsSyncedDesc: Forms are discovered automatically from your WordPress plugin, or you can trigger a manual sync from the ACTV TRKR settings page in WordPress.
forms.allFormsArchived: All forms are archived. Click "Archived" above to view them.
forms.installPlugin: Install the WordPress plugin and sync your forms to start tracking leads.
forms.formCategoryDesc: Categorize this form so you can differentiate lead types in your dashboard.
forms.estimatedLeadValueDesc: Set the estimated dollar value of each lead from this form. Used for lead scoring and weighted reporting.
forms.leadWeightDesc: Control how much this form contributes to your overall lead count and conversion rate. A weight of 1× means full contribution. Set to 0× to exclude entirely.
forms.archiveFormDesc: Archiving hides this form from your dashboard and leaderboards. Existing data is preserved.
forms.archiveFormDescArchived: This form is archived. It won't appear in dashboards or leaderboards. Unarchive it to restore.
forms.noLeadsYet: No leads for this form yet.

monitoring.title: Site Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: Uptime, health, and alerts across all sites.
monitoring.noSites: No sites connected yet. Add a site in Settings.
monitoring.noBrokenLinks: No broken links detected. Run a scan to check.
monitoring.noDomainData: No domain data yet. Click "Check Now" above.
monitoring.noSslData: No SSL data yet. Click "Check Now" above.
monitoring.pluginUpdateMonitoring: Plugin update monitoring will alert you when installed plugins have new versions available.
monitoring.vulnerabilityDesc: Cross-references installed plugins against known vulnerability databases.
monitoring.coreUpdateDesc: Monitors WordPress core version and alerts when updates are available.
monitoring.pluginMonitoringNote: Plugin and WordPress monitoring requires ACTV TRKR plugin v1.4+. These features will activate automatically once your plugin is updated.
monitoring.noFormsForSite: No forms discovered for this site yet.
monitoring.livenessNote: The plugin checks each form's page hourly to verify the form HTML is still present. Forms behind logins or modals may not be detected.
monitoring.formChecksWillUpdate: Form health checks will update shortly.
monitoring.wpPluginOutdated: The WordPress plugin on this site is outdated or inactive; update/re-activate it, then retry.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: WordPress security monitoring for {{orgName}}
security.connectSite: Connect a site in Settings to enable security monitoring.
security.loginAlertsDesc: Failed login attempts, brute force activity, and new IP logins.
security.noSuspiciousActivity: No suspicious login activity detected. The plugin monitors failed attempts automatically.
security.fileChangeDesc: Unexpected file modifications in WordPress core, theme, and plugin files.
security.fileIntegrityDesc: File integrity scans run daily. No unexpected modifications found.
security.alertsAfterFailed: Alerts trigger after 5+ failed login attempts within 10 minutes
security.newIpFlagged: Successful logins from new IPs are flagged for review
security.scansDaily: Scans core files, active theme, and plugin directories daily
security.alertsOnChanges: Alerts on new, modified, or deleted files outside normal update cycles
security.bruteForceInstallPlugin: Install a rate-limiting or login-lockout plugin (e.g. Limit Login Attempts Reloaded or Wordfence)
security.bruteForceEnable2fa: Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) for all admin accounts
security.bruteForceChangeUrl: Change the default /wp-admin login URL using a plugin like WPS Hide Login
security.bruteForceStrongPasswords: Ensure all admin passwords are 16+ characters with mixed case, numbers, and symbols
security.bruteForceBlockIp: Block the attacking IP addresses at your hosting firewall or via .htaccess
security.bruteForceWaf: Consider enabling Cloudflare or a WAF to filter malicious traffic upstream

settings.title: Settings
settings.noApiKeys: No API keys yet. Generate one to get started.
settings.sitesAutoAppear: Sites connected via the WordPress plugin will appear here automatically.
settings.removeSiteConfirm: Remove {{domain}}? This won't delete historical data.
settings.noSitesInstallPlugin: Click "Add Site" above or install the WordPress plugin. The site will appear here once registered.
settings.newApiKeyNotice: New API key — copy it now, it won't be shown again:
settings.apiKeyGeneratedDesc: Copy it now — it won't be shown again.
settings.pluginDownloadDesc: Download the plugin, upload it to WordPress, then paste your API key in Settings → ACTV TRKR.
settings.notificationsDesc: Choose how you want to be notified about new leads and activity.
settings.realtimeLeadEmailsDesc: Get an email instantly when a new form submission arrives
settings.leadEmailDigestDesc: Receive a periodic summary of new leads
settings.browserPushDesc: Desktop notifications for new leads (requires permission)
settings.browserNotSupported: Your browser doesn't support push notifications.
settings.enableInBrowser: Enable notifications in your browser settings.
settings.appearsInHeaders: Appears in report headers and exports
settings.logoFormats: PNG, JPG, SVG, or WebP. Max 2MB.
settings.removeBranding: Remove ACTV TRKR branding from reports
settings.removeBrandingDesc: When enabled, your client logo and name replace ACTV TRKR branding on PDF report exports.
settings.dashboardNotChanged: Your in-app dashboard and navigation are not changed.
settings.colorAppliedNote: Applied to PDF report headers, charts, and accent elements only.
settings.formsAutoDesc: Forms are automatically discovered by the plugin. Use "Sync Forms" in your WordPress plugin settings to rescan. Archive forms you no longer want to track.
settings.noActiveFormsSettings: No active forms discovered yet. Install the plugin and click 'Sync Forms' in WordPress.
settings.formRestoredDesc: The form is now visible in your dashboard.
settings.formArchivedDesc: The form has been hidden from your dashboard.

websiteSetup.title: Set Up Your Website
websiteSetup.subtitle: Download the plugin, connect your site, and start sending data to your ACTV TRKR dashboard.
websiteSetup.downloadPluginDesc: Download the ACTV TRKR WordPress plugin and add it to your website.
websiteSetup.installActivateDesc: Upload the plugin in WordPress, activate it, and open the ACTV TRKR settings.
websiteSetup.connectSiteDesc: Copy your license key, paste it into the plugin, and verify your website connection.
websiteSetup.copyKeySecurityInfo: For security, copy your API key from Settings → API Keys. The key is only shown once when generated.
websiteSetup.instruction1: Download the ACTV TRKR plugin from this page.
websiteSetup.instruction2: In WordPress, go to Plugins → Add Plugin → Upload Plugin.
websiteSetup.instruction3: Upload the plugin zip file and activate it.
websiteSetup.instruction4: Open ACTV TRKR settings inside WordPress (Settings → ACTV TRKR).
websiteSetup.instruction5: Copy your unique license key from this dashboard (Settings → API Keys).
websiteSetup.instruction6: Paste the key into the plugin and click Save Changes.
websiteSetup.instruction7: Wait for connection confirmation — the plugin will automatically register your site.
websiteSetup.instruction8: Once connected, your site will begin sending tracking and lead data to ACTV TRKR.
websiteSetup.needHelpDesc: Contact support if you run into setup issues. We're happy to walk you through the process.
websiteSetup.keySecurityNotice: For security, full API keys are only shown once when generated. Visit Settings → API Keys to generate a new key.
websiteSetup.noApiKeyDesc: Go to Settings → API Keys to generate your license key. You'll need it to connect the plugin.
websiteSetup.noWebsiteConnectedDesc: Download the plugin, install it on your WordPress site, and paste your API key to get started.
websiteSetup.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect {{domain}}? Historical data will be preserved.
websiteSetup.websiteLimitReached: You've reached your website limit ({{allowedWebsites}}). Upgrade your plan to connect additional websites.
websiteSetup.noWebsitesConnected: No websites connected. Complete the setup steps above to get started.
websiteSetup.accountProtectedDesc: Your plugin download can only be used when connected to an active ACTV TRKR account with a valid license key. This helps protect account access and ensures each subscription is tied to approved websites only. The plugin itself does not contain sensitive credentials — access is controlled through your unique API key and account status.
websiteSetup.gettingStartedEmailDesc: After purchase, customers receive a welcome email with their login link and setup instructions. This Website Setup page is your permanent source of truth for downloads, install steps, and connection details — it's always accessible from your dashboard.

traffic_up.title: Traffic is growing
traffic_up.explanation: Sessions increased {{change}}% compared to the previous period.

traffic_down.title: Traffic declined
traffic_down.explanation: Sessions dropped {{change}}% compared to the previous period.
traffic_down.action: Review top landing pages and traffic sources for changes.

lead_growth.title: Lead volume is up
lead_growth.explanation: Leads increased {{change}}% compared to the previous period.

lead_drop.title: Leads declined
lead_drop.explanation: Leads dropped {{change}}% compared to the previous period.
lead_drop.action: Check form health and top lead sources for anomalies.

conversion_gain.title: Conversion rate improved
conversion_gain.explanation: Conversion rate improved {{change}}% compared to the previous period.

conversion_drop.title: Conversion rate dropped
conversion_drop.explanation: Conversion rate declined {{change}}% compared to the previous period.
conversion_drop.action: Review landing pages and form experience for friction.

high_exit_rate.title: High exit rate
high_exit_rate.action: Review page content and calls to action.

mobile_dropoff.title: Mobile users convert less
mobile_dropoff.explanation: Mobile conversion rate is significantly lower than desktop.
mobile_dropoff.action: Review the mobile experience on key landing pages.

form_abandonment.title: Form abandonment is high
form_abandonment.action: Simplify form fields or check for errors.

seo_visibility_loss.title: SEO score declined
seo_visibility_loss.action: Run an SEO scan to identify new issues.

high_intent_low_performance.title: High traffic, low conversions
high_intent_low_performance.explanation: {{page}} gets significant traffic but converts below average.
high_intent_low_performance.action: Review CTA placement and page clarity.

strong_engagement_low_visibility.title: Strong engagement, low visibility
strong_engagement_low_visibility.explanation: {{page}} has high engagement but low traffic.
strong_engagement_low_visibility.action: Consider promoting this page to drive more traffic.

site_health_issue.title: Site health issue
site_health_issue.action: Check the Monitoring page for details.

getStarted.title: Get Started in 3 Simple Steps
getStarted.subtitle: Set up tracking on your WordPress site in under 5 minutes.
getStarted.step1Desc: Log into your ACTV TRKR account and download the WordPress plugin.
getStarted.step1Detail2: Go to Plugins → Add New → Upload Plugin
getStarted.step1Detail3: Upload the ACTV TRKR file and click Activate
getStarted.step2Desc: After activating the plugin, you'll see the ACTV TRKR settings panel.
getStarted.step2Detail1: Copy your API Key from your ACTV TRKR dashboard
getStarted.step2Detail2: Paste it into the plugin settings
getStarted.step2Note: Your site is now linked and ready to start tracking.
getStarted.step3Title: Watch Your Data Come to Life
getStarted.step3Desc: Within minutes, ACTV TRKR begins collecting:
getStarted.step3Note: Return to your dashboard anytime to see what's happening and what needs attention.
getStarted.downloadSuccess: Plugin downloaded! Upload it to WordPress → Plugins → Add New → Upload.

goals.title: Conversion Goals
goals.description: Define what counts as a conversion — forms, clicks, page visits, or custom events.
goals.cvrExplanation: Conversion Rate reflects the actions you've marked as meaningful conversions, including forms, clicks, and key page completions.
goals.conversionHelp: Only goals marked as conversions are included in the main Conversion Rate metric.
goals.conversionValueHelp: Optional estimated dollar value per completion. Used in future weighted reporting.
goals.emptyHint: Create a goal to start tracking conversions beyond form submissions.
goals.cvrTooltip: Conversion Rate = tracked leads ÷ tracked sessions. Form CVR = tracked form submissions ÷ tracked sessions. Goal CVR = non-form goal completions ÷ tracked sessions. Imported leads and submissions from untracked pages are excluded so the rate reflects only activity our tracker actually observed.
goals.cvrFallback: Tracked form submissions ÷ tracked sessions (no goals configured)
goals.cvrFallbackExplainer: Conversion Rate is currently based on tracked form submissions. Create goals to customize what counts.
goals.untrackedLeadsExcluded_one: {{count}} lead excluded from CVR — imported or submitted from an untracked page.
goals.untrackedLeadsExcluded_other: {{count}} leads excluded from CVR — imported or submitted from untracked pages.

rule.urlNote: Use one — contains is more flexible, exact is stricter.
